risk_name: Wholesale Electricity Price Deviation from Financial Model Assumptions

description.summary: Wholesale electricity prices deviate significantly from the project financial model's long-term assumption. Electricity is 70-75% of green hydrogen OPEX, making this the single largest financial exposure. This risk is inherent, well-understood, and managed through PPAs and hedging — not eliminated.
description.detailed: At €40/MWh and 55 kWh/kg (PEM), electricity contributes ~€2.20/kg H₂. If prices average €50/MWh, LCOH increases ~€0.55/kg — potentially erasing project margin. Price deviation sources: wholesale market structural changes, PPA counterparty risk, renewable capture price cannibalization, grid tariff changes. The 2021-2023 European energy crisis demonstrated extreme price scenarios (€200-400/MWh). This risk is almost certain over a 20-year project life but has near-perfect detectability (real-time market prices, forward curves, PPA negotiations).
description.root_cause: Electricity markets are structurally volatile. Factors outside project control: fuel prices, carbon prices, renewable build-out rates, policy, weather.
trigger_events: 12-month rolling wholesale price exceeds model assumption by >15%
PPA counterparty credit rating downgraded below investment grade

assessment.probability_rationale: 100% of operating projects experience electricity price deviation from original model. Almost Certain by definition.
assessment.impact_rationale: Significant NPV impact (€100M+ at 100 MW over 20 years) but managed through PPAs and hedging. Symmetric risk (prices can go down). Competitors face same exposure.
assessment.detectability_rationale: Perfect detectability: real-time wholesale prices, forward curves, regulatory announcements. No other risk has better visibility.

preventive_actions.description: Secure long-term fixed-price PPA for >70% of expected electricity consumption. Blend baseload PPA (minimum production) and indexed PPA (flexible production).

corrective_actions.description: Implement hedging program: 80% hedge ratio years 1-3, 60% years 4-5, 40% years 6-10, spot thereafter.

monitoring_indicators.indicator: 12-month rolling average wholesale price vs. financial model

incident_descriptions: 2021-2023 European energy crisis: Wholesale prices reached €200-400/MWh. Projects with fixed-price PPAs survived. Projects exposed to wholesale prices suspended operations or delayed FID. This crisis demonstrated that PPA coverage is more important than technology choice for financial resilience.

sources.title: IEA World Energy Outlook 2025 — Electricity Market Projections
sources.author: IEA
